equipment.description: Large cylindrical steel vessel containing the nuclear reactor core.
Heat exchanger used to convert water into steam from heat produced in a nuclear reactor core.
Large centrifugal pump used to circulate water through the primary loop of a pressurized water reactor.
Component in a PWR primary circuit that maintains the high pressure of the reactor coolant.
Tank designed to receive and condense steam discharged from pressurizer relief valves.
Electromechanical device that controls the insertion and withdrawal of control rods in the reactor core.
High-pressure pump used in the Chemical and Volume Control System to inject coolant into the primary system.
Pump used to transfer concentrated boric acid solution for reactivity control.
Heat exchanger in the CVCS that cools the primary coolant letdown flow.
Heat exchanger used to recover heat from the letdown flow to warm the charging flow.
Pump used to circulate coolant for decay heat removal during reactor shutdown.
Heat exchanger used to cool the primary coolant during shutdown operations.
High-head pump used for emergency core cooling system injection during a loss-of-coolant accident.
Pump used to spray water into the containment building to reduce pressure and temperature following an accident.
Pressurized vessel storing borated water for passive injection into the reactor core during a large break LOCA.
Large, fast-closing valve located on the main steam lines to isolate the steam generators from the turbine.
Valve on the pressurizer used to prevent overpressurization of the primary system.
Large pump used to supply feedwater to the steam generators.
Pump used to provide feedwater to the steam generators when the main feedwater system is unavailable.
Pump used to remove condensed water from the main condenser hotwell.
Component between the high and low-pressure turbines that removes moisture and reheats steam.
Large steam turbine coupled with an electrical generator to produce electrical power.
Large heat exchanger that condenses exhaust steam from the main turbines back into water.
High-capacity pump used to circulate cooling water from the heat sink through the main condenser.
Pump supplying cooling water to critical safety-related heat exchangers.
Heat exchanger that transfers heat from various nuclear plant components to the essential service water system.
Pump used to circulate water through the spent fuel pool cooling system to remove decay heat.
Heat exchanger used to remove decay heat from the spent fuel pool water.
Standby generator providing backup electrical power to essential safety systems during a loss of offsite power.
Large DC battery bank providing uninterruptible power to vital instruments and controls.
Inverter that converts DC battery power to AC power for vital instruments and control systems.
Electrical distribution equipment used to route power to large plant motors and transformers.
Assembly of motor starters and breakers for controlling lower voltage plant equipment.
Transformer that steps down main generator voltage to supply power to plant auxiliary systems.
Temperature sensor located just above the reactor core to monitor coolant exit temperature.
Detector located outside the reactor vessel used to measure neutron flux for reactor power monitoring.
Detector inserted into the reactor core to map the local neutron flux distribution.
Instrument used to measure the flow rate of coolant in the primary loop.
Instrument used to measure the water level inside the pressurizer vessel.
Instrument used to monitor the pressure inside the reactor containment building.
Instrument used to measure hydrogen concentration in the containment atmosphere following an accident.
Detector used to monitor radiation levels in plant areas or process streams.
Filter used to remove particulate matter from the boric acid solution.
Vessel containing ion exchange resins used to remove dissolved impurities from the reactor coolant.
Tank used in the CVCS to provide a surge volume and a point for adding hydrogen to the primary coolant.
Large tank used to temporarily store radioactive liquid waste for decay before processing.
Component used to concentrate radioactive impurities in liquid waste through evaporation.
Tank used to store radioactive gases to allow for decay before controlled release.
Large fan-coil unit used to cool and dehumidify the air inside the containment building.
Refrigeration machine used to provide chilled water for the control room air conditioning system.
High-efficiency particulate air filter used in ventilation systems to prevent the release of radioactive dust.
Bed of activated charcoal used in ventilation systems to remove radioactive iodine from the air.
